title: sclarea pratensis
intro: Sclarea pratensis, also known as wild clary or wild sage, is a perennial plant belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae) and is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant's medicinal properties are primarily attributed to its essential oil, which contains compounds like camphor, linalool, and borneol, and has been used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of wild clary include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties, which can aid in relieving symptoms of colds, coughs, and respiratory issues. Wild clary can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or essential oils for topical use, and is often used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
